name: H3 (Hexagonal Hierarchical Geospatial Indexing)

agent_readiness.notes: Hexagonal hierarchical spatial indexing system by Uber. h3.latlng_to_cell(lat, lng, resolution). h3.cell_to_latlng(cell). Resolutions 0-15 (0=largest hexagon covering area, 15=~1m²). k_ring(cell, k) for neighbors. compact/uncompact for multi-resolution. polyfill for polygon. geo_to_h3/h3_to_geo. Python, JavaScript, Go, Java bindings. Used in ride-sharing (Uber's surge pricing), delivery routing, GIS analysis, spatial joins. Binds lat/lon points to hexagonal cells for aggregation.

gotchas: H3 v4 API changes from v3 (function names changed)
Resolution choice is critical — test multiple resolutions
Edge artifacts at pentagon cells (12 exist globally)
JavaScript (h3-js) has slight API differences from Python
best_when: Geospatial data aggregation at multiple zoom levels
Nearby entity search and service area calculation
Spatial joins at hexagonal grid level
Any app aggregating GPS data into grid cells
avoid_when: Rectangular grid requirements (S2 or Geohash may suit better)
summary: H3 is Uber's hexagonal hierarchical geospatial indexing system. latlng_to_cell(lat, lng, resolution) maps coordinates to hexagonal cells at 16 resolution levels. k_ring for neighbors, polyfill for polygon coverage. Python, JavaScript, Go, Java. Used for spatial aggregation, surge pricing, delivery routing. Ideal for aggregating GPS data into scalable grid cells.
